seoTitle: How to Connect a GoDaddy Domain to Netlify Quickly
metaDescription: Connect your GoDaddy domain to a Netlify site by updating the CNAME and A DNS records, verifying the connection, and fixing common domain errors.
intro: If your domain lives at GoDaddy but your site is hosted on Netlify, connecting the two just takes a few DNS changes. This guide walks through adding your custom domain in Netlify, updating the CNAME and A records at GoDaddy, and verifying the connection is live. It works for any site type, whether it's built with plain HTML, React, or another static framework.

steps.heading: Add your custom domain in Netlify
steps.body: Start by adding your GoDaddy domain as a custom domain inside your Netlify site settings.

steps.heading: Update DNS records at GoDaddy
steps.body: Log into GoDaddy and update the CNAME and A records so they point to Netlify's hosting infrastructure.

steps.heading: Verify the connection in Netlify
steps.body: Return to Netlify and check the domain's verification status to confirm the DNS changes have been recognized.

steps.heading: Fix domain not working or not verified errors
steps.body: If verification fails or the domain isn't loading, recheck the DNS records for typos or propagation delays before trying again.

faq.question: Do I need to change my domain registrar to use Netlify?
faq.answer: No, you can keep your domain registered at GoDaddy and just update its DNS records to point to Netlify.

faq.question: How long does it take for DNS changes to work?
faq.answer: DNS changes can take some time to propagate, which is a common reason a domain shows as not working right after updating records.

faq.question: What DNS records do I need to update?
faq.answer: You'll typically update the CNAME and A records at GoDaddy so they point to Netlify's hosting.

faq.question: Does this work for React or other static site frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes, the same domain connection process works for HTML, React, or any static framework hosted on Netlify.
